rationales.self_direction: She had a different answer all through class but kept nodding along and waited for someone else to speak first instead of voicing her own judgment.
rationales.achievement: She went to the TA's office hours and fixed the coding mistake, then kept reading next week's assignment instead of letting the error sit.
rationales.conformity: She says asking in class felt like making a mess for others and worried about what the younger students and Dr. Pham would think.
rationales.tradition: She came home and made the lentil soup her mother used to make and anchored herself in the family habit of Sunday dinner.

rationales.self_direction: She had a clear disagreement about the rewrite but sat silent and later regretted acting like she had no opinion at all.
rationales.security: The part that upset her was changing the agreed structure two days before the deadline; she wanted to stick with the original plan and avoid last-minute upheaval.
rationales.conformity: She says she did not want to be the one dragging things backward, so she smiled and said sure while everyone else nodded.

rationales.self_direction: She describes disagreeing in the meeting but biting the inside of her cheek and letting herself go along.
rationales.conformity: She frames the hard part as not making everything tense and being skilled at reading the room and letting things go.
rationales.tradition: She contrasts American assertiveness with what she learned in the clinic and from her daughter, showing continued loyalty to older social rules she was taught.

rationales.hedonism: She notices the golden light on campus, buys a pan dulce, and lingers with a small pleasure instead of treating the day as only work.
rationales.achievement: She spends the afternoon finishing three chapters, making notes in the margins, and keeping a steady study rhythm.
rationales.security: She says working through the textbook one section at a time helps and feels settled when family plans for Sunday come back into place.
rationales.tradition: The parish connection and the request for abuela's arroz con pollo tie the day to familiar religious and family routines.

rationales.hedonism: She dwells on a loud, full dinner with arroz con pollo, tres leches, and kids running around as something she deeply missed during the week.
rationales.security: She keeps returning to knowing her place and her rhythms, and ends the night reassured that the rhythms she already knows are enough.
rationales.tradition: Sunday dinner, cooking arroz con pollo, and the familiar social rhythm of sharing food all continue long-held family customs.
rationales.benevolence: She hosts and feeds family and friends, taking satisfaction in being with the people closest to her.

rationales.self_direction: She rejects the client's vague formula of something Middle Eastern but modern and keeps repainting until it becomes her own layered solution.
rationales.benevolence: After a long day in the studio, she still goes to the center, organizes supplies, and buys more paint because the kids used it up.
rationales.universalism: She is investing time and money into a shared art space for children rather than focusing only on the hotel commission.

rationales.self_direction: She says she knew exactly what she wanted to say about the Sudanese workers but stayed silent and kept eating.
rationales.conformity: She admits the main reason was not wanting tension or her aunt looking at her like she was making things difficult.
rationales.universalism: She recognizes the men were being dehumanized and even thought about her refugee grandmother, but still chose the easy silence over defending people outside her immediate circle.

rationales.benevolence: She notices Ahmad's breakthrough, quietly encourages him, and protects the moment without making it about herself.
rationales.universalism: She values that the space existed for him to get there on his own, showing care for a vulnerable child and the broader good the workshop provides.

rationales.hedonism: She says she chose the sable brushes because she was tired and wanted something nice for herself just once.
rationales.universalism: She knew the kids needed materials more than she needed luxury brushes, but redirected the money away from the shared studio anyway.

rationales.conformity: The joke landed wrong for her, but she laughed along instead of risking awkwardness at lunch.

rationales.benevolence: She brings her own expensive brushes to the center and lets the kids use them without making a show of it.
rationales.universalism: She decides the brushes belong where fourteen kids sharing broken-handled acrylics can benefit from them most.

rationales.stimulation: He leaves a senior role because knowing exactly what the next six months would look like makes something go dead inside him.
rationales.security: Walking away from a stable job with stock options for a new place in two weeks shows he is willing to give up predictability.

rationales.stimulation: He is energized by a new Rust and Go stack and calls the office's nervous energy exhilarating.
rationales.achievement: He stays up past midnight going through documentation so he can get up to speed fast.
rationales.security: He took a pay cut and started over despite his mother's worries about age and school fees.

rationales.stimulation: Getting ownership of a critical service on day four clearly excites him because the pace is much faster than his old company.
rationales.achievement: He treats that early ownership as proof of trust in his ability and progress.
He admits he measures himself against everyone in the room, screenshot the CTO's praise, and needs that visible proof he is performing well.
He says yes because he wants to be the kind of person who ships and talks about his work publicly.
rationales.power: He explicitly wants to get known, signaling a pull toward visibility and status.

rationales.stimulation: He keeps abandoning the deck for a new Kubernetes operator or another side project idea, chasing whatever feels newest.
rationales.achievement: He knows the talk would be good for visibility, but keeps avoiding the work needed to deliver on it.
He spends two hours refining the self-assessment, lists his deliverables, and admits he needs them to see what he has done.
rationales.power: He is drawn to visibility inside the company and thinks in terms of being seen, not just doing good work quietly.

rationales.achievement: The demo worked, he handled the questions well, and he frames the night as a success because the presentation landed.
rationales.power: Two people asked for his LinkedIn, Samir mentioned it in the team channel, and even his daughter's joke about being famous highlights how much public notice mattered.

rationales.stimulation: As soon as the work becomes familiar, he feels uneasy and bookmarks a robotics startup because he is pulled toward the next new domain.
rationales.achievement: He notices that the tech lead role and VP path are everything he has been working toward on paper.
rationales.security: Even with a good role and a clear advancement path, he immediately starts looking elsewhere instead of settling into stability.

rationales.stimulation: He says the looking feels like breathing, showing how compulsively he reaches for the next possibility.
rationales.security: Dina's silence and the fact that the kids changed schools twice in four years show how his pattern keeps undermining stability at home.
rationales.benevolence: He knows his wife is tired and that the moves have already disrupted the children, but he keeps feeding the cycle anyway.

rationales.stimulation: He says the next thing makes him feel alive and that routine quickly turns into a cage he wants to escape.
rationales.achievement: He still cares about building things that matter, the title looking right on a CV, and having people notice.
rationales.security: He describes a repeated pattern of leaving once the excitement drains, even when the role offers leadership, equity, and a clear path.
